- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

Shell commands must end with \n. Special keys must be sent alone without \n, e.g. {"keystrokes":"C-c","duration":0.5}.

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings

def normalize_text(x):

]:

import pandas as pd, re, os

def normalize_text(x):
    if isinstance(x, list):
        x = ' '.join(str(s) for s in x)
    x = str(x).lower()
    x = x.replace('\r', ' ').replace('\n', ' ').replace('\t', ' ')
    x = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', x).strip()
    return x

os.makedirs('/app/work', exist_ok=True)
for split, src, dst in [
    ('train', '/app/data/train-00000-of-00001.parquet', '/app/work/train.txt'),
    ('test', '/app/data/test-00000-of-00001.parquet', '/app/work/test.txt'),
]:
    df = pd.read_parquet(src)
    with open(dst, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
        for label, text in zip(df['label'].astype(int).tolist(), df['text'].tolist()):
            f.write(f'__label__{label} {normalize_text(text)}\n')
    print(split, df.shape, '->', dst, os.path.getsize(dst))
PY
python3 /app/prepare_fasttext_data.py

I need to wait for autotune to finish, then verify the produced model size and local test accuracy, remove the `.vec` if present, and copy a qualifying model to `/app/model.bin`.
Plan: Start a single bounded polling loop for up to 15 minutes checking PID 4838 and `/app/work/model_aut2.bin`. When the process is finished and a nonzero model binary exists, print the autotune log tail, remove `.vec`, list model files, evaluate local accuracy with `/opt/fastText/fasttext test`, and if accuracy >=0.62 and size <150MB copy the model to `/app/model.bin`. If no valid model is produced, print a clear fallback message.

arguments.keystrokes:
cat > /app/work/wait_eval_copy.sh <<'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
set -u
PIDFILE=/app/work/autotune2.pid
LOGFILE=/app/work/autotune2.log
MODEL_BIN=/app/work/model_aut2.bin
MODEL_FTZ=/app/work/model_aut2.ftz
TESTFILE=/app/work/test.txt
FINAL=/app/model.bin
MAX_WAITS=30
WAIT_INTERVAL=30
MAX_SIZE=150000000
MIN_ACC=0.62

echo "=== Waiting for autotune to finish ==="
for i in $(seq 1 "$MAX_WAITS"); do
  pid=$(cat "$PIDFILE" 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ -n "$pid" ] && [ -d "/proc/$pid" ]; then
    printf '(%s/%s) pid=%s running; ' "$i" "$MAX_WAITS" "$pid"
    tail -1 "$LOGFILE" 2>/dev/null || true
    sleep "$WAIT_INTERVAL"
    continue
  fi
  if [ -s "$MODEL_BIN" ] || [ -s "$MODEL_FTZ" ]; then
    echo "autotune finished and model file exists"
    break
  fi
  printf '(%s/%s) finished but no model file; ' "$i" "$MAX_WAITS"
  tail -1 "$LOGFILE" 2>/dev/null || true
  sleep "$WAIT_INTERVAL"
done

echo "=== Autotune log tail ==="
tail -40 "$LOGFILE" 2>/dev/null || true

echo "=== Clean/check model files ==="
rm -f /app/work/model_aut2.vec
ls -lh /app/work/model_aut2.* 2>/dev/null || echo 'No model files found'

if [ -s "$MODEL_BIN" ]; then
  MODEL="$MODEL_BIN"
elif [ -s "$MODEL_FTZ" ]; then
  MODEL="$MODEL_FTZ"
else
  echo 'No valid autotune model produced; fallback needed'
  exit 1
fi

SIZE=$(stat -c %s "$MODEL")
echo "Selected model: $MODEL size=$SIZE bytes"

echo "=== Evaluate local test accuracy ==="
/opt/fastText/fasttext test "$MODEL" "$TESTFILE" 2>&1 | tee /app/work/eval_aut2.txt || echo 'test failed'

echo "=== Parse accuracy ==="
awk -v min="$MIN_ACC" '/^P@1/ {acc=$3+0; print "parsed_accuracy="acc; if (acc >= min && acc > 0) {print "accuracy_ok"; exit 0} else {print "accuracy_too_low"; exit 1}}' /app/work/eval_aut2.txt
code=$?  # In bash, this captures the awk exit code.

echo "=== Copy to /app/model.bin if size and accuracy OK ==="
if [ "$SIZE" -lt "$MAX_SIZE" ]; then
  if [ -s /app/work/eval_aut2.txt ] && awk -v min="$MIN_ACC" '/^P@1/ {acc=$3+0; if (acc >= min && acc > 0) {exit 0} else {exit 1}}' /app/work/eval_aut2.txt; then
    cp "$MODEL" "$FINAL"
    echo "copied to $FINAL"
    ls -lh "$FINAL"
    echo "final_size_bytes=$(stat -c %s "$FINAL")"
    echo "FINAL_OK"
  else
    echo "accuracy below $MIN_ACC; not copying"
    echo "AUTOTUNE_ACCURACY_TOO_LOW"
  fi
else
  echo "model size $SIZE >= $MAX_SIZE; not copying"
  echo "AUTOTUNE_SIZE_TOO_LARGE"
fi
SCRIPT
chmod +x /app/work/wait_eval_copy.sh

Analysis: The autotune run finished but produced only a 0-byte `/app/work/model_aut2.ftz` and no valid `.bin`, so `/app/model.bin` has not been created. The terminal is back at a clean prompt. We need a clean manual fallback that trains, evaluates, quantizes if necessary, and saves a valid model to `/app/model.bin` under 150MB with local test accuracy >=0.62. To avoid terminal pollution from interactive polling, I will run one foreground script that manages the whole workflow with logging and bounded waits.
Plan: Create and run `/app/work/final_train.sh`. It will kill stray fastText processes, clean stale artifacts, train a supervised fastText model on `/app/work/train.txt` with a balanced configuration, poll for completion inside the script, evaluate on `/app/work/test.txt`, quantize with retraining if the raw model is over 135MB, re-evaluate the quantized model, and copy the first qualifying model to `/app/model.bin`. It will print clear status and final verification at the end.

arguments.keystrokes:
cat > /app/work/final_train.sh <<'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
set -u
FSTEXT=/opt/fastText/fasttext
TRAIN=/app/work/train.txt
TEST=/app/work/test.txt
BASE=/app/work/model_final
FINAL=/app/model.bin
MAX_SIZE=150000000
QUANT_TRIGGER=135000000
MIN_ACC=0.62
SEED=42

echo "=== final_train.sh start $(date -Iseconds) ==="

# Kill stray fasttext supervised processes
for p in /proc/[0-9]*; do
  cmd=$(tr '\0' ' ' < "$p/cmdline" 2>/dev/null)
  case "$cmd" in
    *fasttext*) echo "killing ${p##*/}: $cmd"; kill -KILL "${p##*/}" 2>/dev/null || true;;
  esac
done
sleep 2

# Clean stale artifacts
rm -f "$BASE".bin "$BASE".vec "$BASE".ftz /app/work/train_final.log /app/work/train_final.pid /app/work/eval_final.txt /app/work/quantize_final.log /app/work/model_final_quant.ftz
rm -f /app/work/model_aut2.bin /app/work/model_aut2.vec /app/work/model_aut2.ftz /app/work/autotune2.log /app/work/autotune2.pid /app/work/eval_aut2.txt
rm -f "$FINAL"

echo "=== Data check ==="
wc -l "$TRAIN" "$TEST"
df -h /app /app/work | tail -5

echo "=== Training supervised model ==="
nohup "$FSTEXT" supervised \
  -input "$TRAIN" \
  -output "$BASE" \
  -dim 100 \
  -epoch 25 \
  -lr 0.7 \
  -wordNgrams 2 \
  -minn 3 \
  -maxn 5 \
  -bucket 200000 \
  -minCount 2 \
  -loss softmax \
  -thread "$(nproc)" \
  -seed "$SEED" \
  > /app/work/train_final.log 2>&1 &
train_pid=$!
echo "$train_pid" > /app/work/train_final.pid
echo "train_pid=$train_pid"

# Poll for completion up to 25 minutes
for i in $(seq 1 50); do
  if [ -d "/proc/$train_pid" ]; then status=running; else status=finished; fi
  size=$(stat -c %s "$BASE.bin" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
  printf '(%s/50) status=%s bin_size=%s ' "$i" "$status" "$size"
  tail -1 /app/work/train_final.log 2>/dev/null || true
  if [ "$status" = finished ] && [ "$size" -gt 0 ]; then echo 'training_done'; break; fi
  if [ "$status" = finished ] && [ "$size" -eq 0 ]; then echo 'training_finished_no_model'; break; fi
  sleep 30
done

if [ ! -s "$BASE.bin" ]; then
  echo "ERROR: no valid raw model produced"
  tail -40 /app/work/train_final.log 2>/dev/null || true
  exit 1
fi

rm -f "$BASE.vec"
RAW_SIZE=$(stat -c %s "$BASE.bin")
echo "raw_model_size=$RAW_SIZE"

echo "=== Evaluating raw model ==="
"$FSTEXT" test "$BASE.bin" "$TEST" 2>&1 | tee /app/work/eval_final.txt
RAW_ACC=$(awk '/^P@1/ {print $3+0}' /app/work/eval_final.txt 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
echo "raw_accuracy=$RAW_ACC"

if [ "$RAW_SIZE" -lt "$MAX_SIZE" ] && awk -v min="$MIN_ACC" '/^P@1/ {exit !($3+0 >= min)}' /app/work/eval_final.txt; then
  cp "$BASE.bin" "$FINAL"
  echo "copied raw model to $FINAL"
else
  echo "raw model not acceptable; raw_size=$RAW_SIZE raw_accuracy=$RAW_ACC"
  if [ "$RAW_SIZE" -gt "$QUANT_TRIGGER" ] || ! awk -v min="$MIN_ACC" '/^P@1/ {exit !($3+0 >= min)}' /app/work/eval_final.txt; then
    echo "=== Quantizing with retrain ==="
    "$FSTEXT" quantize \
      -input "$BASE.bin" \
      -output "$BASE" \
      -qnorm -retrain -cutoff 100000 \
      -thread "$(nproc)" \
      > /app/work/quantize_final.log 2>&1
    echo "quantize_exit=$?"
    tail -30 /app/work/quantize_final.log 2>/dev/null || true
    if [ ! -s "$BASE.ftz" ]; then
      echo "ERROR: quantization did not produce $BASE.ftz"
      exit 1
    fi
    QUANT_SIZE=$(stat -c %s "$BASE.ftz")
    echo "quantized_size=$QUANT_SIZE"
    echo "=== Evaluating quantized model ==="
    "$FSTEXT" test "$BASE.ftz" "$TEST" 2>&1 | tee /app/work/eval_final.txt
    QUANT_ACC=$(awk '/^P@1/ {print $3+0}' /app/work/eval_final.txt 2>/dev/null || echo 0)
    echo "quantized_accuracy=$QUANT_ACC"
    if [ "$QUANT_SIZE" -lt "$MAX_SIZE" ] && awk -v min="$MIN_ACC" '/^P@1/ {exit !($3+0 >= min)}' /app/work/eval_final.txt; then
      cp "$BASE.ftz" "$FINAL"
      echo "copied quantized model to $FINAL"
    else
      echo "ERROR: quantized model not acceptable; quantized_size=$QUANT_SIZE quantized_accuracy=$QUANT_ACC"
      exit 1
    fi
  else
    echo "ERROR: raw model failed accuracy but is not large enough for quantization fallback"
    exit 1
  fi
fi

echo "=== Final verification ==="
ls -lh "$FINAL"
echo "final_size_bytes=$(stat -c %s "$FINAL")"
"$FSTEXT" test "$FINAL" "$TEST" 2>&1 | tee /app/work/eval_final_check.txt
echo "=== final_train.sh done $(date -Iseconds) ==="
SCRIPT
chmod +x /app/work/final_train.sh
/app/work/final_train.sh

#!/bin/bash
set -u
FSTEXT=/opt/fastText/fasttext
TRAIN=/app/work/train_aut.txt
VAL=/app/work/val_aut.txt
OUT=/app/work/grid_results.txt
: > "$OUT"

echo "=== grid_search start $(date -Iseconds) ==="

trials=(
  "-dim 100 -epoch 20 -lr 0.7 -wordNgrams 2 -bucket 100000 -minCount 2 -loss softmax"
  "-dim 120 -epoch 20 -lr 0.5 -wordNgrams 2 -bucket 150000 -minCount 2 -loss softmax"
  "-dim 80 -epoch 25 -lr 0.6 -wordNgrams 2 -bucket 80000 -minCount 3 -loss softmax"
  "-dim 100 -epoch 20 -lr 0.5 -wordNgrams 2 -bucket 100000 -minCount 2 -loss ova"
  "-dim 150 -epoch 15 -lr 0.4 -wordNgrams 2 -bucket 150000 -minCount 2 -loss softmax"
)

best_acc=0
best_name="none"
best_size=0
best_base=""

idx=0
for trial in "${trials[@]}"; do
  idx=$((idx+1))
  name="grid_${idx}"
  base="/app/work/${name}"
  rm -f "$base.bin" "$base.vec" "$base.log"
  echo "=== TRIAL $name args: $trial ==="
  "$FSTEXT" supervised -input "$TRAIN" -output "$base" $trial -minn 0 -maxn 0 -thread "$(nproc)" -seed 42 > "$base.log" 2>&1
  if [ ! -s "$base.bin" ]; then
    echo "FAILED $name" | tee -a "$OUT"
    tail -20 "$base.log" || true
    continue
  fi
  rm -f "$base.vec"
  size=$(stat -c %s "$base.bin")
  eval_out=$("$FSTEXT" test "$base.bin" "$VAL" 2>&1)
  acc=$(echo "$eval_out" | awk '/^P@1/ {print $3+0}')
  echo "$name args=$trial size=$size val_acc=$acc" | tee -a "$OUT"
  echo "$eval_out" | tail -3
  if [ -n "$acc" ] && awk -v best="$best_acc" -v cur="$acc" 'BEGIN{exit !(cur>best)}'; then
    best_acc=$acc
    best_name=$name
    best_size=$size
    best_base="$base.bin"
  fi
done

echo "BEST_NAME=$best_name BEST_ACC=$best_acc BEST_SIZE=$best_size BEST_BASE=$best_base" | tee -a "$OUT"
echo "=== grid_search done $(date -Iseconds) ==="
SCRIPT
chmod +x /app/work/grid_search.sh
